solutions.solution: (c) From the given information, following family diagram can be drawn [diagram showing C as daughter, A as sister, D as father, B at bottom] So, it clear that A is the aunt of B.
(c) From the given information, following family diagram can be drawn [diagram showing A married to C as father, B at bottom] Now, it is clear that A is the mother of B.

(c) By applying above method for (R + D × M + N), we draw the following family diagram [diagram showing B at top with Mother, M as grandson of B, D as father, R as brother, N at bottom] Hence, it is clear that M is the grandson of B.

(e) By applying above decoding method, we can check all the options for the required relationship (a) (M + T – R × F) = Rejected [diagram] J is the uncle of F (b) (J + R – T × F) = Rejected [diagram] J is the grandmother of F (c) (J + M – N × F) = Rejected [diagram] J is the uncle of F So, none of the options is correct to answer the question. So, option (e) will follow.

(b) By applying above decoding method, we can check all the options for the required relationship (a) (M + K × T – R) = Rejected [diagram] Here, gender of R is not known. So, R is either niece or nephew of M. (b) (M – J + R – N) = Selected [diagram] R is the niece of M As, we have already obtained the answer, so there is no need to check other options.
